findings.description: Header hierarchy and title styling are simplified in HTML, reducing distinction between the main title, agenda label, and date/location lines.
findings.evidence: On page 1 PDF, the centered title block uses multiple font sizes and stronger visual separation: 'Marine Fisheries Commission Business Meeting', large 'AGENDA', then 'Teleconference via WebEx' and date centered below. In HTML, these appear as left-aligned heading and separate lines with less pronounced hierarchy.

findings.description: Agenda times and items are converted from aligned schedule formatting into nested bullet lists, changing the visual presentation of the timetable.
findings.evidence: On pages 1 and 2 PDF, times appear in a left-aligned schedule format with agenda text aligned beside them. In HTML, entries such as '9:00 a.m.', '9:10 a.m.', and later items are rendered as bullets with nested bullets rather than a two-part agenda line.

findings.description: Overall page layout is preserved only approximately; the HTML uses a single flowing column with different spacing and indentation from the source schedule layout.
findings.evidence: The PDF on page 1 presents a centered title area followed by a structured agenda with time labels separated from content by horizontal alignment. The HTML preserves sections and order but uses a standard web single-column layout with altered whitespace groupings.

findings.description: Bold emphasis is not preserved consistently for action items and section labels.
findings.evidence: On page 2 PDF, items like 'Vote on final approval of Diamondback Terrapin Management Areas**', 'Repacking of Foreign Crab Meat in North Carolina...', and several rule suspension vote lines are strongly bolded. In HTML, some bold remains, but emphasis appears less consistent and visually weaker across agenda items.

findings.description: Structured agenda formatting with aligned times is flattened into bullets and nested bullets on the second page.
findings.evidence: Page 2 PDF shows times such as 11:15 a.m., 12:15 p.m., 1:15 p.m., 2:30 p.m., 3:00 p.m., 3:30 p.m., 3:45 p.m., and 4:00 p.m. in a formal schedule layout. The HTML renders these as bulleted list items with nested subitems, changing the visual structure though retaining content.

findings.description: Heading hierarchy does not accurately reflect the PDF structure and introduces heading levels for lines that are not true section headings in the source.
findings.evidence:
PDF top matter shows centered title lines: 'Marine Fisheries Commission Business Meeting', 'AGENDA', 'Teleconference via WebEx', 'May 14, 2020' as formatted title text, not a nested heading hierarchy. HTML encodes these as

Marine Fisheries Commission Business Meeting

,

AGENDA

,

Teleconference via WebEx

,

May 14, 2020

, which imposes a structure not clearly present in the PDF.

findings.description: Footer guidance text is duplicated in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF includes footer note once per page: '* Times indicated are merely for guidance. The commission will proceed through the agenda until completed. **Probable Action Items' with page numbers 1 and 2. HTML repeats this note near the top as list text ending with '**Probable Action Items 1', again mid-document as '**Probable Action Items 2', and again at the end as another duplicated list item ending with '**Probable Action Items 2'.

findings.description: Some text is inaccurately transcribed or malformed, including punctuation/spacing and list formatting artifacts that affect fidelity.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'Blue Crab Fishery Management Plan Amendment 3 – Corrin Flora, Joe Facendola'; HTML: 'Blue Crab Fishery Management Plan Amendment 3 –Corrin Flora, Joe Facendola' (missing space). PDF footnote is '* Times indicated are merely for guidance. The commission will proceed through the agenda until completed. **Probable Action Items'; HTML renders malformed list text with page numbers appended: '**Probable Action Items 1' and '**Probable Action Items 2'. PDF section under Director's Report is a clean bulleted list; HTML collapses bullets into a paragraph with markdown-style asterisks: 'Reports and updates on recent Division of Marine Fisheries activities *   Division of Marine Fisheries Quarterly Update *   Recreational Hook and Line Modifications...'.
